content: 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems can be redesigned to use a recursive 'agent within memory' architecture, where each stored episodic memory chunk is itself a lightweight active learning agent that volunteers to be retrieved when it detects new input relevant to its own learned domain of interest. This architecture leverages curiosity-driven retrieval incentives rather than static similarity matching to prioritize which memories the core agent accesses for complex sequential decision-making.
